root_cause.summary: cpython's `update_windows_tcltk` branch updated the Windows-bundled tcltk to 9.0.3 (see L229: `Fetching tcltk-9.0.3.0-unsigned...`). The new tcltk returns UTF-8-encoded mojibake instead of correctly-encoded surrogate pairs when Tcl evaluates a string containing a non-BMP code point. Two test_tcl tests fail with the same shape: `tcl.eval(r'set a "<\ud83d\udcbb>"')` returns `'<ðŸ’»>'` (UTF-8 bytes interpreted as Latin-1) instead of the expected surrogate pair `'<\U0001f4bb>'` — `test_eval_surrogates_in_result` (test_tcl.py:57) and `test_evalfile_surrogates_in_result` (test_tcl.py:292). Linux/macOS use system tcl/tk and are unaffected — all non-Windows matrix legs pass. This Windows job exits 2 after a re-run also failed.

evidence_spans.reason: The `Fetching tcltk-9.0.3.0-unsigned...` line confirms this Windows leg downloaded the new tcltk bundle that the branch is updating. Critical context for tying the test failures to the branch's change.

First FAIL block: `FAIL: test_eval_surrogates_in_result`, the file:line in the test source, the failing assertion `tcl.eval(r'set a "<\ud83d\udcbb>"'), '<\U0001f4bb>')`, and the literal `AssertionError: '<\ud83d\udcbb>' != '<ðŸ’»>'` diff (with `- expected / + received` markers underneath). Self-contained — identifies the test name, file, line, the call, and the encoding mismatch.

Second FAIL block: `test_evalfile_surrogates_in_result` at test_tcl.py:292 with the same `'<\ud83d\udcbb>' != '<ðŸ’»>'` shape — establishes the failure is a class of issue (Unicode surrogate handling), not a single-test fluke.

Final test runner summary: `1 re-run test: test_tcl`, `1 test failed: test_tcl`, `Total tests: run=50,658 failures=4`, `Result: FAILURE then FAILURE` (the `then FAILURE` confirms the re-run also failed), and the GHA `##[error]Process completed with exit code 2.`. Confirms the failure is deterministic (re-run did not fix it) and propagated to job exit code.

must_not_claim: the test source code is wrong
